meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: API Integration Guide to Ship Faster
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client projects. Use this API integration guide and AI-powered workflow to ship professional SaaS features in hours, not weeks.
content.answer_block: Stop building from scratch and start orchestrating. By leveraging Claude Code for rapid implementation and Antigravity for standardized API scaffolding, you transform complex integrations into modular, repeatable tasks. This workflow eliminates technical debt, keeps your codebase clean, and ensures you ship polished, production-ready features for your clients every single time.
content.intro:
You’re staring at an empty IDE, the cursor blinking with the rhythmic mockery of a missed deadline. You’ve promised your client a seamless integration, but the documentation is a labyrinth, the authentication flow feels like a security nightmare, and the project has stalled for the third time this week. You aren't failing because you lack skill; you're failing because you're doing manual, low-leverage work.

Professional shipping isn't about writing every line of code—it's about masterfully integrating existing systems into a cohesive product. This guide is your blueprint for breaking the paralysis. We’re replacing manual grind with an AI-augmented pipeline, ensuring you finish projects with the speed of a senior engineer and the precision of a seasoned architect.

steps.title: Define the Integration Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, define your data requirements clearly to prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the API documentation provided. Create a modular TypeScript schema definition for the expected request/response payloads to ensure type safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate interface definitions for [API Name] based on the attached documentation.'

steps.title: Establish Secure Auth Handshake
steps.description: Handle credentials using environment variables to keep client data secure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure authentication helper module using best practices for Bearer token management and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement an Axios interceptor for [API Name] authentication with automatic token refresh logic.'

steps.title: Develop Service Layer Wrappers
steps.description: Encapsulate API calls into clean service functions for easy maintenance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service class that abstracts the API endpoints into clean, reusable methods with built-in retry logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor these API calls into a service layer with standardized error handling and logging.'

steps.title: Implement Mock Testing
steps.description: Ensure your integration works even when the API is down or rate-limited.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate mock response fixtures based on the API documentation for unit testing the service layer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate Jest test suite for the new API service layer using the provided mock data.'

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Finalize the code and document the integration for your client's handover.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a concise README section for this API integration, detailing setup, environment variables, and usage examples.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a developer-friendly README.md section explaining how to configure and use this integration.'

pro_tips: Always wrap your API calls in a circuit breaker pattern to prevent system-wide crashes during external outages.
Use 'dry-run' modes in your integration logic to test payloads without hitting production endpoints.
Never hardcode base URLs; use a centralized config file that pulls from .env files for environment-specific switching.
Implement request logging that strips sensitive PII (Personally Identifiable Information) before hitting your logs.
Force your AI to write unit tests *before* the implementation code to ensure the logic remains decoupled and testable.
content.next_steps: Once your first integration is live, build a template repository for your most common API patterns. This will allow you to clone your 'perfect' setup for every new client project, cutting your development time by 70%.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting technical friction hold your freelance career hostage. Start building with the velocity of a top-tier firm. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
